details: UNDEFINED DEFECT
Explanation
This trouble code is displayed when an undefined trouble code has been stored in the trouble code memory.
Note: If the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) program was selected manually. check again that the correct program has been selected.
If the correct program has been selected or if selection took place automatically, data has been stored in the trouble code memory for some
unknown reason.
The trouble code can be ignored if there are no apparent defects in the system.
Defect Code 1:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 1 TR signal/EKP relay:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when a short occurs at the EKP relay or on the control wire to the DME control unit Pin 1. This defect code is
only stored when another defect has already been detected.
Effects of defect: The fuel pump relay is not activated. The EGS control unit receives no TR signal.
Troubleshooting: The TR signal (engine speed signal once per revolution) is output, when the engine is running, at Pin 1 of the DME control unit and is
routed via the electrical fuel pump relay to the EGS control unit.
Possible causes: Wires, connectors or electronic fuel pump relay defective.
Defect Code 3:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 3 Injector valves cyl. 1+3:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when shorts or breaks occur at the fuel injector valves cyl. 1+3 or on their supply wires.
Effects of defect: The entire group of injector valves is always deactivated when a short occurs at one injector valve or on its supply wire. The two other
cylinders remain in operation.
Troubleshooting:
Activate Injector valves (see ‹›D‹› Component Activation) as indicated on display screen.
Check connections and wires in accordance with schematic.
Replace failed injector valves
For test values, see - Component Testing.
AIR FLOW SENSOR
Explanation
This fault is stored when shorts or breaks occur on the Volume Air Flow Sensor (VAF) or its leads (power supply or signal lead). Also recognition
when Potentiometer Voltage (Up)/Supply Voltage (Uv) value of VAF is no longer in the normal operating range of 0.02 to 0.95 V.
NOTE: The Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit works with determined replacement values, Load Signal (tL)=3.5 ms (partial load) and
6.0 ms (full load) and ignition timing = 20 crankshaft degrees before TDC when this defect is detected.
Troubleshooting
Check signal by means of status monitoring. Up/Uv value shows the relation between potentiometer voltage (slider) and power supply voltage
(5 V) in air flow sensor.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 8:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 8 Fault lamp:
Explanation: Only applies to US models. This defect code is stored when shorts occur at the fault lamp or on its supply wire.
Effects of defect:
The fault lamp does not light in the case of a short to positive when defect codes are stored.
The fault lamp lights continuously in the case of a short to ground.
Troubleshooting: Normally, the fault lamp is activated by the control unit (ground signal at Pin 8) when an emission-related defect code is stored in the
defect code memory.
Fault lamp test: Simulate defect, e.g. disconnect plug from air flow meter and start engine. The fault lamp must then light when the control unit detects
the defect code 41 air flow meter.
Does the control unit send the ground signal?
Are wires and fault lamp OK.?
Defect Code 12
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 12 Throttle potentiometer:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when a break or shorts occur at the throttle potentiometer or on its supply wires.
Effects of defect:
Increased idle speed
Restricted operation, delayed reaction to accelerator pedal operation and loss in performance
Troubleshooting: Check throttle potentiometer with ‹›D‹›2 Status monitoring. Press accelerator pedal and observe throttle value, idle, part load and full
load signals. The voltage value must increase uniformly when the accelerator pedal is depressed.
The idle setting of the throttle potentiometer must be detected as the idle speed. In the case of automatic transmission, full load must be detected just
before the kick-down.
If not: Disconnect DME control unit. The control unit then relearns the throttle idle voltage.
KNOCK SENSOR CYL. 1 & 2
Explanation
This trouble code is stored in the case of a break or short at:
Wire from Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit to the knock sensor.
Defective knock sensor.
Troubleshooting
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
CAMSHAFT POSITION SENSOR
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when the pulse generator signal is interrupted or faulty.
Troubleshooting
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Check signal with oscilloscope. Connect universal adapter to engine wire harness. The signal is output once per camshaft revolution.
DIFFERENTIAL AIR INTAKE SYSTEM
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when a break or short to the Differential Air Intake System (DISA) change-over valve is detected.
Troubleshooting
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Check for defective change-over valve and wiring.
IDLE ACTUATOR CLOSING CIRCUIT
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when a break or short occurs at:
Idle actuator
Output stage in the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit
The trouble code 29 is additionally stored in the case of a break in the voltage supply or plug-and-socket connection to the idle actuator.
NOTE: trouble code 29 can also be stored when engine operation is interrupted abruptly without the ignition being switched off beforehand. The
defect is then detected at an engine speed < 600 rpm.
Troubleshooting
Perform component activation with tester. The idle actuator is then activated at one second intervals. If there is no response, connect test
adapter 61 1 491 or equivalent between the wire harness and idle actuator. A voltage of approx. 12 V should be measured at terminal 2. The
value between terminal 1/2 is >10 V.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions if applicable.
INJECTOR VALVE CLY. 2 & 4
Explanation
Procedure as for detect code 3.
Defect Code 36:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 36 Fuel evaporation control valve:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when a short occurs at the fuel evaporation control valve or on its supply wire.
Effects of defect:
Short to ground: No fuel evaporation control since the solenoid valve remains closed.
Short to positive (or break): The solenoid valve constantly remains open. As a result, the fuel vapors supplied via the fuel evaporation control
valve can lead to malfunctions when idling and during acceleration.
Troubleshooting: Activate TE-valve with ‹›D‹›3 Component activation. Does the TE-valve respond?
YES: Check wire and connector for loose contact.
NO: Connect test lamp (12 V 10 W) and activate component. Does test lamp flash?
YES: Check function of TE-valve.
NO: Check voltage supply at TE-valve. Check wires. Check output at digital motor electronics control unit (Pin 36).
See - Component Testing.
OXYGEN SENSOR HEATING
Explanation
This trouble code is stored in the case of a break or short at:
Oxygen sensor (heater pan)
Sensor heating relay
Wire from Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit to sensor heating relay
Wires to oxygen sensor
Output stage in the DME control unit
Troubleshooting
Perform component activation with tester. The relay is activated at 1 second intervals - this is audible. In the case of defect "connection to
sensor signal", check whether the defect is at the sensor or in its supply wire by monitoring the oxygen sensor.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
AIR FLOW SENSOR
Explanation
This fault is stored when shorts or breaks occur on the Air Flow Sensor (VAF [LMM]) or its leads (power supply or signal lead). Also recognition
when Potentiometer Voltage (Up)/Supply Voltage (Uv) value of LMM is no longer in the normal operating range of 0.02 to 0.95 V.
NOTE: The Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit works with determined replacement values, Load Signal (tL)=3.5 ms (partial load) and
6.0 ms (full load) and ignition timing = 20 crankshaft degrees before TDC when this defect is detected.
Troubleshooting
Check signal by means of status monitoring. Up/Uv value shows the relation between potentiometer voltage (slider) and power supply voltage
(5 V) in air flow sensor.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
KNOCK SENSOR CYL. 1 & 2
Explanation
This trouble code is stored in the case of a break or short at:
Wire from Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit to the knock sensor.
Defective knock sensor.
Troubleshooting
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 48:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 48 A/C compressor cut-out:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when a short occurs at Pin 48.
Effects of defect:
Short to positive: Below a speed of 8 km/h, the A/C compressor is not switched off during acceleration with full load.
Short to ground: The A/C compressor does not operate.
Troubleshooting: Check A/C compressor cut-out relay and supply wires
CONTROL UNIT SUPPLY VIA MAIN RELAY
Explanation
This trouble code is stored if a break occurs in the supply voltage after start or when the voltage value is outside the permissible range between 10
V and 14 V.
NOTE: If a break occurs, the supply is provided internally in the control unit via terminal 30.
Troubleshooting
If the voltage was outside the set range, check the alternator, voltage regulator and the battery.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 64:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 64 Ignition timing Intervention:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when a short to ground occurs on the wire from the electronic transmission control to the DME control unit Pin
Effects of defect: In such a case, ignition timing intervention only occurs once, the signal is then ignored. The automatic transmission is in the
emergency operation program.
Troubleshooting:
Check wire and connector to the electronic transmission control.
Perform defect code monitoring on the electronic transmission control. Monitor status once again after test run.
OXYGEN SENSOR
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when a short or a break occurs at the oxygen sensor or on its supply wires. The prerequisite for this is an engine
temperature of >158°F (>70°C) over a period of 3 minutes and load signal of 3.5 ms for 5 seconds..
NOTE: The system uses a substitute value of 0.45 V until the defect is corrected.
Troubleshooting
Check the oxygen sensor signal at operating temperature with status monitoring. The voltage value should fluctuate between 0.02 and 0.85 V.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
VEHICLE SPEED SIGNAL
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when the road speed signal is not plausible while driving. The prerequisite for this is an engine speed >2500 rpm at
high load for at least 10 sec.
Troubleshooting
If the indicated speed (speedometer) is "OK", check wire to the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit. If no faults are found, check the
instrument cluster.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 76:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 76 Idle CO potentiometer
Explanation: Only applicable to vehicles without catalytic converter. This defect code is stored when shorts or breaks occur at the potentiometer or on
its supply wire.
Effects of defect: A substitute value of 2.77 V is set. The emission CO value may deviate from the nominal value.
Troubleshooting:
Read out CO potentiometer actual value with ‹›D‹›2 Status monitoring.
Check CO potentiometer (in LMM) and supply wires.
Measure CO content in exhaust emission.
For test values see - Component Testing.
INTAKE AIR TEMPERATURE SENSOR
Explanation
This trouble code is stored in the case of a short or break at the temperature sensor or on its supply wires. A prerequisite for detecting a break is a
time interval of 3 min. after start and idling for at least 30 sec.
NOTE: After defect detection, a substitute value of 122°F (50°C) is used.
Troubleshooting
Check temperature values by means of status monitoring. If not plausible: Check wire to Digital Motor Electronic (DME) Pin 77 and
temperature sensor.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
ENGINE TEMPERATURE SENSOR (COOLANT)
Explanation
This trouble code is stored in the case of a short or break occurring at the temperature sensor or on its supply wires. To detect a short to positive or
a break, the plausibility of the coolant and the intake air temperature is checked. When this defect is detected the system will use substitute values
to continue engine operation.
Troubleshooting
Check temperature values by means of status monitoring.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
A/C PRESSURE SIGNAL
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when the A/C compressor is sensed as being switched "ON" (battery voltage on pin 85) but no A/C switch signal (0 V
on pin 86) is received simultaneously.
Troubleshooting
Check wires to the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit Pin 85 and 86 for a short and break.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 100:
‹›D‹›1 Defect code memory: 100 Output stage:
Explanation: This defect code is stored when the function of an output stage is not plausible and the control unit could not distinctly identity the output
stage. If, at the same time, one of the following components is stored as being defective, then this is the cause of the defect code 100 being set and can be
ignored:
Idle actuator
Injector valves
Sensor heating relay
Fuel evaporation control valve
Fault lamp (US only)
EKP relay
Break at Pin 54
Troubleshooting: Activate the corresponding components with ‹›D‹›3 Component activation or check wires.
DME CONTROL UNIT
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when a defect is detected in the RAM or in the ROM/EPROM.
Troubleshooting
After clearing the trouble code memory, carry out a test run. If the defect occurs once again, disconnect the Digital Motor Electronic (DME)
control unit from the wire harness for approx. 5 min in order to trigger a reset. The control unit must be replaced if the defect is repeated.
EMISSION CONTROL
Explanation
This trouble code is stored when the emission control reaches its control limit and no other defect which effects the mixture has been detected.
This may be attributed to deviations in the mixture composition (mixture too rich or too lean for longer than 10 sec).
Possible causes
Vehicle stops due to lack of fuel (tank empty)
Incorrect fuel pressure
Defective or coked injector valves
Incorrect output value from engine temperature sensor
Secondary air in intake or exhaust tract
Defect in tank ventilation
Poor combustion (ignition, inlet/exhaust valves, compression etc. defective...)
NOTE: Poor engine response will occur since the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) operates with a substitute value.
Troubleshooting
Measure CO content at exhaust manifold while observing the oxygen sensor voltage.
Establish operating condition at the time the defect was detected.
Proceed with displayed scan tool diagnostic instructions where applicable.
Defect Code 300:
Malfunction: Engine operation not possible:
Prerequisites:
Starting system OK. (battery charged, Ignition switch...)
Ground connection from battery to engine or body OK.
Specified fuel in tank
Engine OK. mechanically (timing, compression...)
Troubleshooting:
a) Check voltage supply to DME control units in accordance with schematic.
b) Measure Input signals.
Check engine speed signals from the pulse generators at starting speed
Check air mass meter signals ‹›D‹›200.
C) Measure output signals.
Drive fuel pump relay in accordance with schematics
Ignition output stages by means of ENGINE TEST test step 09
Injector valves with ‹›D‹›300
d) Check fuel pressure
e) Check electronic throttle control for defects
For technical data refer to - Component Activation
Basic Descriptions
LEGEND
1211 - Control Module (DME)
DME Terminal Info
DME Terminal Info
Cancel the DME control unit fault code memory by disconnecting the control unit harness connector (control unit located in the glove Box); see
COMPONENT LOCATIONS. Reconnect the DME control unit connector and test drive the vehicle.
If a fault code 1211 is present, the DME control unit is defective. Always refer to CHECKING THE BASICS before replacing the control
unit.
1215 - Air Flow Meter
Disconnect the harness connector from the air flow meter.
Engine Management
With the ignition switch in the run position, measure the following voltages at the female connector.
Gray/white wire (terminal 1) and ground = Approx. 5 volts
Gray/white and gray/blue wire (terminals 1 and 5) = Approx. 5 volts
Gray/white and gray/yellow wire (terminals 1 and 2) = Approx. 5 volts
If any voltage is incorrect, check and repair associated connections and wires for opens and shorts to voltage and ground. Refer to SCHEMATIC
DIAGRAMS.
If wires and connections are OK, replace the DME control unit.
If all voltages are correct check for continuity between the following terminals at the air flow meter connector (male plug). Measure between pins:
41 and 14 = Continuity
41 and 59 = Continuity
If any results are incorrect, replace the air flow meter.
If all results are correct, proceed to step 5.
Reconnect the harness connector to the air flow meter and remove cover from rear of connector.
Measure the voltage between gray/yellow wire (terminal 2) and ground while moving the intake air flap manually.
If voltage varies between approximately 0.5 and 4.5 volts, check or repair wire to terminal 41 of DME control unit harness connector for an
open circuit. If wire is OK, replace the DME control unit.
If the voltage does not vary between 0.5 and 4.5 volts, check or repair wire to terminal 41 of DME control unit harness connector for short to
ground. If wiring is OK, repair or replace the air flow meter.
1221/1222 - Oxygen Sensor
Engine Management
If a code 1221 or 1222 is present, cancel the DME fault code memory after noting all other existing codes.
Start the engine and warm it up to normal operating temperature. Drive the vehicle varying the load conditions. If the "Check Engine" light does
not illuminate the problem is intermittent. If the "Check Engine" light does illuminate and a fault code 1221 or 1222 is present continue with step
With the ignition switch in the OFF position, disconnect the oxygen sensor in-line connector C140.
Turn the ignition switch to the run position and make the following voltage measurements at connector C140 (male side).
Black wire (terminal 2) and ground = approx. 0.65 volts.
Black (terminal 2) and brown wire (terminal 1) = approx. 0.45 volts.
Turn the ignition switch OFF and measure between:
Black wire (terminal 2), and brown wire (terminal 1) = 0 volts.
If all voltages are correct, continue with step 6.
If any voltages are incorrect, check wires to terminals 70 and 71 of the DME control unit (harness disconnected), for an open or short to
ground. If the wires are OK, the DME control unit may be defective.
Check the following items for operation and repair or replace as necessary. If no problems are found continue with step 7.
RUNNING RICH
Engine oil diluted with fuel
Operation of the purge valve
Operation of the charcoal canister
Operation of the DME main relay
Operation of the fuel pump relay
Stuck open injectors
Operation of the of the fuel pressure regulator
RUNNING LEAN
Air leak at crankcase vent hose
Air leak at the idle control valve hoses
Air leak at the rubber boot between the air flow meter and throttle housing
Air leak at injector rubber sealing rings
Clogged injectors
Insufficient fuel pressure
Bad fuel (water in fuel)
With the ignition switch OFF, install a new oxygen senor.
Reconnect harness connectors, start the engine, and warm it up to normal operating temperature. Drive the vehicle varying the load conditions. If
the "Check Engine" light does not illuminate, retain the new oxygen sensor. If the "Check Engine" light does illuminate and a fault code 1221 or
1222 is present the DME control unit is defective.
1223 - Coolant Temperature Sensor
NOTE:
Perform this test only if MCU code 1223 was retrieved from Digital Motor Electronic (DME) memory.
Engine Management
1. With the ignition switch in the OFF position, disconnect the coolant temperature sensor connector.
2. Turn the ignition switch to the run position and make the following voltage measurements at the connector (female half).
Brown/red wire (terminal 1) and ground = approx. 5 volts.
If not check wire to terminal 78 of the DME control unit harness connector for an open or short to voltage or ground. If wire is OK, the DME
control unit is defective.
Brown/red (terminal 1) and brown/black (terminal 2) = approx. 5 volts. If not, check wire to ground for an open.
If all voltages are correct, continue with step 3.
3. With the ignition switch in the OFF position, clear the DME control unit memory by disconnecting the DME control unit harness connector.
Reconnect the control unit harness connector.
4. Install a new coolant temperature sensor.
5. Start the engine and warm it up to normal operating temperature, varying the load conditions.
If the check engine light remains off, retain the new coolant temperature sensor. End of test.
If the "Check Engine" light illuminates, repeat the self diagnostic procedures in "EXTRACTING FAULT CODES".
If a code 1223 reoccurs the DME control unit is defective.
1224/2224 - Intake Air Temperature Sensor
Engine Management
With the ignition switch in the OFF position, disconnect the air flow meter connector.
Turn the ignition switch to the run position and make the following voltage measurement at the female portion of the connector.
Gray/violet wire (terminal 4), and ground = approx. 5 volts. If not, check wire to terminal 77 of at the DME control unit for an open or short
to voltage or ground. If wire is OK, the DME control unit is defective.
If the voltage is correct continue with step 3.
With the ignition OFF, disconnect the DME control unit connector; This cancels the DME fault codes. Be sure to take note of all fault codes
before cancellation.
Install a new air flow meter, (known good unit).
Reconnect all harness connectors, start the engine and warm it up to operating temperature.
If the "Check Engine" light does not illuminate retain the new air flow meter.
If the "Check Engine" light illuminates and a fault code 1224 is present the DME control unit is defective.
1231 - Battery Voltage
Engine Management
1. With the engine running check power supply to the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit.
Red/white wire (terminal 54) = approx. 13.0-14.5 volts. If not check for alternator output and voltage regulator operation, possible voltage
drop at the DME main relay and wiring, and insufficient V-belt tension. Repair or replace component(s) as necessary.
If the power supply is "OK" and MCU code 1231 registers in DME memory, the DME control unit may be defective.
1251 - Fuel Injectors 1 and 3
Engine Management
Engine Management
DME Connector View
1. With the ignition switch in the "OFF" position, disconnect the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit harness connector (control unit is
located in the E-Box). See COMPONENT LOCATIONS.
2. Connect a FUSED jumper between brown wire (terminal 27), of the DME control unit connector and ground.
3. Turn the ignition switch to the run position and MOMENTARILY connect a FUSED jumper between brown/white wire (terminal 3), of the DME
control unit connector and ground.
4. Listen and feel fuel injectors 1-3.
If both fuel injector valves click, remove fused jumpers. Replace the DME control unit.
If one or more fuel injection valves do not click, remove FUSED jumper at brown/white wire (terminal 3).
5. With the ignition switch in the OFF position, disconnect harness connectors from fuel injector valves 1 and 3.
6. Turn the ignition switch to the run position and measure the voltages of each fuel injector connector.
Red/White wire (terminal 1) and ground = Battery voltage. If not check wire to the DME main relay for open.
Brown/white (terminal 2) and red/white wire (terminal 1) = 0 volts. If not check Brown/white wire to terminal 3 of DME control unit
connector for a short to ground.
Brown/white wire (terminal 2) and ground = 0 volts. If not check wire to terminal 3 of the DME control unit connector for a short to voltage.
7. Reconnect a FUSED jumper between terminal 3 of the DME control unit harness connector and ground. Measure the voltage at the fuel injector
connector.
Brown/white (terminal 2) and red/white wire (terminal 1) = battery voltage. If not check brown/white wire to terminal 3 of connector for an
open.
If all the voltages are correct, remove fused jumpers and replace the defective fuel injector(s).
1252 - Fuel Injectors 2 and 4
Engine Management
Engine Management
DME Connector View
1. With the ignition switch in the "OFF" position, disconnect the Digital Motor Electronic (DME) control unit harness connector (control unit is
located in the E-Box). See COMPONENT LOCATIONS.
2. Connect a FUSED jumper between brown wire (terminal 27), of the DME control unit connector and ground.
3. Turn the ignition switch to the run position and MOMENTARILY connect a FUSED jumper between brown/white wire (terminal 32), of the DME
control unit connector and ground.
4. Listen and feel fuel injectors 2-4.
If both fuel injector valves click, remove fused jumpers. Replace the DME control unit.
If one or more fuel injection valves do not click, remove FUSED jumper at brown/white wire (terminal 32).
5. With the ignition switch in the OFF position, disconnect harness connectors from fuel injector valves 2 and 4.
6. Turn the ignition switch to the run position and measure the voltages of each fuel injector connector.
Red/White wire (terminal 1) and ground = Battery voltage. If not check wire to the DME main relay for open.
Brown/white (terminal 2) and red/white wire (terminal 1) = 0 volts. If not check Brown/white wire to terminal 32 of DME control unit
connector for a short to ground.
Brown/white wire (terminal 2) and ground = 0 volts. If not check wire to terminal 32 of the DME control unit connector for a short to voltage.
7. Reconnect a FUSED jumper between terminal 32 of the DME control unit harness connector and ground. Measure the voltage at the fuel injector
connector.
Brown/white (terminal 2) and red/white wire (terminal 1) = battery voltage. If not check brown/white wire to terminal 32 of connector for an
open.
If all the voltages are correct, remove fused jumpers and replace the defective fuel injector(s).
1263 - Purge Valve
Engine Management
1. With the engine at normal operating temperature, disconnect the purge valve connector and connect a voltmeter between terminals 1 and 2 of the
female side of the connector.
2. Start the engine a run at 1800 rpm for 60 seconds.
If the voltmeter cycles between battery and 0 volts and code 1263 is stored in Digital Motor Electronic (DME) memory, replace the purge
valve.
If the voltmeter reads 0 volts continuously, go to step 3.
If the voltmeter reads battery voltage continuously, check or repair brown wire from terminal 2 of the purge valve connector to terminal 36 of
the DME control module harness connector for a short to ground. If a short to ground is not present, the DME control module is defective.
3. With the ignition switch "OFF", disconnect the DME control unit connector.
4. Connect a fused jumper between terminal 36 of harness connector and ground.
5. Connect a fused jumper between terminal 27 of harness connector and ground.
6. Turn the ignition switch to the "RUN" position, and measure the voltage at the purge valve harness connector.
If the voltmeter reads battery voltage, remove the fused jumpers. The DME control unit is defective.
If battery voltage is not present, remove the fused jumpers and check the wires for opens and the DME main relay for proper operation.
Repair or replace as necessary.
With AST 5500
*** FOR UPDATED INFORMATION NOT INCLUDED HERE, REFER TO TSB# 72 01 90 (3008) DATED JANUARY 1990 AND # 72 04 91
Flash Codes
TROUBLE CODE TABLE
Three digit numeric flash codes are converted to two digit numeric trouble codes with description and possible remedy. After code(s) have been
identified proceed to Trouble Code Diagnosis. See: Manufacturer Code Charts/Restraint Systems/With AST 5500
With BMW or BMW Compatible Equipment
*** THIS ARTICLE REFLECTS CHANGES MADE BY TSB# 72 01 90 (3008), DATED JANUARY 1990 AND # 72 04 91 (3388) DATED MAY
Three digit numeric flash codes are converted to two digit numeric trouble codes below. After code(s) have been identified proceed to Trouble Code
Diagnosis. See: Manufacturer Code Charts/Restraint Systems/With BMW or BMW Compatible Equipment
3-4-1 = None
4-2-3 = None
4-4-1 = None
Engine Controls System
Defect Code Table:
‹›D‹› Defect code memory - stored defect codes:
Defect codes stored in defect code memory -> Defect code 0 to defect code 204
See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Engine Control System/With - BMW DIAGNOSTIC SYSTEM
Malfunctions - Defect codes not stored:
Engine operation not possible -> Defect code 300
See: Diagnostic Trouble Code Descriptions/Engine Control System/With - BMW DIAGNOSTIC SYSTEM/Code 300 (Engine Operation Not Possible)
Starting inrush of NC compressor too high -> Defect code 301
Sporadic cut-out when idling -> Defect code 301
No idle speed increase -> Defect code 301
Engine cannot be turned off when air conditioning switched on -> Defect code 301
Flash Code 4-2-3
*** THIS ARTICLE REFLECTS CHANGES MADE BY TSB# 72 04 91 (3388) DATED MAY 1994 ***
PYROTECHNIC SAFETY BELT TENSIONER
Correction
If the 4-2-3 flash code is encountered, the electrical connections should be checked. If there are no electrical problems found, the tensioner unit is
either deployed or defective and must be replaced. No attempt should be made to disassemble or service the tensioner unit.
Flash Code 4-2-3
*** THIS ARTICLE REFLECTS CHANGES MADE BY TSB# 72 04 91 (3388) DATED MAY 1994 ***
PYROTECHNIC SAFETY BELT TENSIONER
Correction
If the 4-2-3 flash code is encountered, the electrical connections should be checked. If there are no electrical problems found, the tensioner unit is
either deployed or defective and must be replaced. No attempt should be made to disassemble or service the tensioner unit.
